artifact.content_preview:
NGIS PHASE 3 — SPRINT REVIEW MEETING MINUTES
Meeting Date: March 3, 2026
Time: 13:00–14:30 EST
Location: WebEx / Room 4C-12, Place du Portage III, Gatineau

Attendees:
- Alice Tran (Project Manager) — Chair
- Bob Morrison (Technical Lead)
- Carol Nguyen (Infrastructure Architect)
- Dave Chen (Security Specialist)
- Marie Lefebvre (Change Management)
- Farida Osei (QA Lead)

Regrets: Director Okafor (represented by Alice Tran)

---

1. SPRINT 6 REVIEW (Feb 17 – Mar 3, 2026)

Completed Items:
- AD

extracted_tasks.text: must be initiated immediately given his role as the
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: must be initiated immediately given his role as the

extracted_tasks.text: - AD Schema Extension deployed to test environment (Bob Morrison)
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: - AD Schema Extension deployed to test environment (Bob Morrison)

extracted_tasks.text: - Network topology design finalized for Sites B and C (Carol Nguyen)
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: - Network topology design finalized for Sites B and C (Carol Nguyen)

extracted_tasks.text: - User acceptance testing scripts drafted (Farida Osei)
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: - User acceptance testing scripts drafted (Farida Osei)

extracted_tasks.text: - Legacy application inventory completed — 14 apps catalogued (Dave Chen)
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: - Legacy application inventory completed — 14 apps catalogued (Dave Chen)

extracted_tasks.text: - PAW architecture documentation for SA&A package (blocked — see Item 3)
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: - PAW architecture documentation for SA&A package (blocked — see Item 3)

risks.description: Bob Morrison, the sole Active Directory migration expert, may leave SSC within 60 days. His departure would create a critical single point of failure with no documented successor, jeopardizing all remaining migration sprints and the integrity of the AD migration wizard he developed.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Bob Morrison has indicated he is exploring external opportunities and may be leaving SSC within 60 days. Knowledge transfer plan must be initiated immediately given his role as the sole AD migration e

risks.description: The migration script depends on the deprecated 'legacy-sync' API endpoint scheduled for decommission on April 1, 2026. If refactoring is not completed before that date, the migration pipeline will break entirely, halting all production cutover activity.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The current migration script relies on the deprecated 'legacy-sync' API endpoint scheduled for decommission on April 1. Bob to assess refactoring effort.

risks.description: TBS Audit has identified an ITSG-33 IA-5 compliance finding due to PAW architecture documentation being omitted from the SA&A package. Failure to remediate by March 12 could result in authority-to-operate being withheld, blocking production deployment.

evidence_refs.excerpt: TBS Audit identified an ITSG-33 IA-5 finding... Dave Chen confirmed the PAW documentation was inadvertently omitted from the SA&A package.

risks.description: Cisco Catalyst 9500 switch delivery has been delayed to March 20, two days after the March 18 migration window for Site A. This threatens the scheduled cutover and may cascade into downstream sprint timelines and contractual commitments.

evidence_refs.excerpt: TechSupplier has confirmed a 2-week delay on Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches. Revised delivery is March 20. This threatens the March 18 migration window.

risks.description: The end-user communication plan is only 60% complete and the UAT test plan has not yet been distributed, creating a hidden risk that end users and stakeholders will be inadequately prepared for the migration, increasing the likelihood of resistance, errors, and post-cutover incidents.

evidence_refs.excerpt: End-user communication plan (in progress — 60% complete)... Farida Osei to complete and distribute UAT test plan to all stakeholders for review. Deadline: March 10, 2026.
